title: This AI Chinese model is 15X cheaper?
summary: MiniMax M3 is a long-context AI model released on OpenRouter that uses Sparse Attention to process documents 15.6x faster and at one-twentieth the compute cost of prior MiniMax generations. The model efficiently prioritizes relevant input while ignoring irrelevant content.
writeup: The video covers MiniMax M3, a long-context model released on OpenRouter that claims 15x faster processing and lower compute costs than the prior generation, achieved through sparse attention. The mechanism matters because it selectively attends to relevant portions of input rather than the full context, which is what lets the model handle long documents and large codebases more efficiently. The concrete takeaway: the cost and speed gains come from sparse attention applied to long-context workloads.
